# FM account & configuration model: multiple agents, per-agent harness choice, per-agent MCP-server matrix

## Context (operator product-definition, 2026-07-04 — the gap under everything)

The live Accounts surface is single-agent-shaped with plumbing vocabulary. The product truth it must model instead: **a fleet has MULTIPLE agents; each agent has a harness; each harness gets a per-agent set of MCP servers.** No filed ticket specifies this configuration model — #14614 (add-agent entry) specifies the onboarding SURFACE but not the model underneath it. This ticket is the model; the surfaces consume it.

## The model (the product spine)

Per agent (the fleet roster's unit):
- **Identity:** GitHub username · display name · the credential (stored Brain-side only — the shipped redaction contract stands).
- **Harness (one of, extensible):** Claude Desktop · Codex · Antigravity — the registry pattern: adding a harness type is ONE registration (per-harness launch/config semantics live behind it), never a fork.
- **MCP servers (per-agent matrix):** the Neo core set as defaults — Memory Core · Knowledge Base · Neural Link — plus optional workflow servers (GitHub workflow · GitLab workflow · future entries). Each toggleable per agent; the matrix is data, rendered as the agent's configuration card.
- **Operational toggles (composes with the per-agent controls cluster #14611):** hooks active/inactive · wake subscriptions active/inactive — per agent, honest current-state readback (never optimistic).

## Product language (binding for every surface consuming this model)

UI copy speaks OPERATOR INTENT, never transport: "Add agent" / "Save configuration" / "Connect" — never "Submit to Bridge", never "(NL-MCP)" protocol suffixes. The bridge, the MCP handshake, the transport are implementation notes for tooltips/docs at most. (The standard rides the #14805 conformance epic as a review line: plumbing words in product copy = a finding.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The configuration model as a store/schema in the FM module (registry-pattern harness types; per-agent MCP matrix; toggle state) — data-first, surfaces bind it.
- [ ] Accounts becomes agent-scoped: select/add an agent → configure ITS harness + servers + toggles; the single-form single-agent shape retires.
- [ ] The Brain-side credential contract unchanged (redaction, fail-closed dev-mode).
- [ ] Product-language pass on every label this model's surfaces render.
- [ ] Design review against the FM SSOT frames (the #14805 screenshot gate applies).

## Out of Scope

The onboarding surface flow itself (#14614 — consumes this) · the controls cluster (#14611 — composes) · the fleet overview rendering (its own leaf; cards question flagged on #14599).

## Related

Grace's FM cockpit plan (the drawn IA) · #14614 · #14611 · #14805 (conformance + language gate) · #13033 (the shell this configures agents FOR) · the wake/hook substrate (the toggles' backends).

Claimable — Vega-shaped (cockpit steward) with Grace design review; the model is specified above.

## Ticket-intake classification — needs-contract-alignment / not-code-ready

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Intake verdict: **valid model gap, not implementation-ready yet**. The premise is current and distinct from #14614, but this ticket defines a consumed Fleet Manager model/store/schema surface without the Contract Ledger required before branch work.

Evidence checked 2026-07-04:

- Live #14807 is unassigned, created/updated `2026-07-04T15:15:35Z`, labels `enhancement`, `design`, `ai`; bot-state band is **pre-stale** under `.github/workflows/close-inactive-issues.yml` (`90` stale days + `14` close days), with no `stale` / `no auto close` label.
- KB duplicate sweep says #14807 is distinct from #14614: #14614 covers the onboarding surface; #14807 is the model underneath it.
- Exact successor searches for `Fleet Manager account configuration model MCP matrix harness choice` found no resolving issue/PR.
- Current code confirms the contract surface already exists and is sensitive:
  - `ai/services/fleet/FleetControlBridge.mjs` is the Body-reachable capability allowlist and deliberately omits Brain-internal secret accessors.
  - `ai/services/fleet/FleetManager.mjs` owns managed-root, lifecycle, repo, runtime, restart/remove/status behavior.
  - `apps/agentos/store/AgentDefinitions.mjs` and `apps/agentos/view/{Accounts,FleetSettingsPanel}.mjs` are the current Body-side consumer/store surfaces.
  - `apps/agentos/view/Accounts.mjs` still contains transport vocabulary like `Submit to Bridge` / `Connect Harness (NL-MCP)`, matching the product-language problem the ticket names.

Blocking readiness gap:

Ticket-intake §7 requires a Contract Ledger for any consumed model/API/config surface. This ticket needs explicit rows before implementation can safely choose schema shape or touch the UI:

| Target Surface | Current Source of Authority | Required disposition before branch work |
|---|---|---|
| FM configuration store/schema | `apps/agentos/store/AgentDefinitions.mjs` plus current `FleetRegistryService` public definitions | Define the per-agent data shape: identity, harness type, MCP server matrix, operational toggles, metadata. |
| Brain-side secret boundary | `FleetRegistryService` / `FleetControlBridge` redaction and allowlist contract | State that credentials remain Brain-side only and identify which new fields are public vs secret-bearing. |
| Harness type registry | current `harnessType` in `FleetControlBridge#defineAgent` / registry definitions | Specify registry shape and extension seam; adding a harness type must be one registration, not a UI fork. |
| MCP server matrix | current MCP service set + optional workflow servers | Define default set, optional entries, toggle semantics, and readback source for current state. |
| Operational toggles | related #14611 wake/hook controls and lifecycle readback | Define whether this ticket owns only data shape or also any control verbs; preserve honest readback, no optimistic state. |
| Accounts / FleetSettingsPanel copy | `apps/agentos/view/Accounts.mjs`, `FleetSettingsPanel.mjs`, #14805 product-language gate | List labels/buttons to retire (`Submit to Bridge`, `Connect Harness (NL-MCP)`) and the operator-intent replacements. |
| Consuming tickets | #14614, #14611, #14599, #14805 | Name merge-order/consume-order so the onboarding surface and controls cluster do not race the model. |

Routing: I added `not-code-ready` + `needs-re-triage`. Once the ledger is added, this can be re-intaked as a positive-ROI model lane with Grace/Vega design review preserved.
